title: Cheilanthes lanceolata
intro: Cheilanthes lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaf lip fern, is a species of fern in the family Woodsiaceae, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and delicate fronds. The lance-leaf lip fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain and protect against cellular damage. The bioactive constituents of Cheilanthes lanceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various ailments, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cheilanthes lanceolata is classified as a species of fern within the genus Cheilanthes, which belongs to the family Pteridaceae. This plant is part of the order Polypodiales. The family Pteridaceae is a family of ferns known to be primarily composed of epiphytic and terrestrial species. The genus Cheilanthes specifically includes species of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions worldwide. Cheilanthes lanceolata is often found growing in areas with high humidity and mild temperatures.
